---
title: "Exploratory Data Analysis of DHS Estimates"
description: "Initial data cleaning, extraction, and analysis of Department of Homeland Security reports."
author: "Mario Arce Acosta"
date: "2026-04-12"
categories: [R, EDA, Data Cleaning]
#image: "dhs_thumbnail.jpg" # Save an image in the same folder to show on the blog grid
---

## The Objective

Before deploying any advanced modeling, it is critical to understand the shape, missingness, and distributions of the raw Department of Homeland Security data.

## Loading the Data

We'll start by pulling in t

```{r}
#| label: data-prep
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(DBI)
library(RPostgres)

# 1. Connect and fetch your highly granular ACS summary
con <- dbConnect(
  RPostgres::Postgres(),
  dbname = "postgres",
  host = "aws-1-us-east-2.pooler.supabase.com", 
  port = 6543,
  user = "postgres.dpuhypyqwupzesfkiplu",
  password = Sys.getenv("SUPABASE_PWD")
)
acs_raw_summary <- dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT * FROM acs_foreign_summary")
dbDisconnect(con)

# 2. Load the DHS Official CSVs
totals <- read_csv("dhs_historical_totals.csv") %>%
  filter(Data_Year >=2009 & Data_Year <= 2019) 
dhs_states <- read_csv("dhs_historical_state.csv") %>%
  filter(Data_Year >=2009 & Data_Year <= 2019) 
dhs_countries <- read_csv("dhs_historical_country.csv") %>%
  filter(Data_Year >=2009 & Data_Year <= 2019) 
dhs_age <- read_csv("dhs_unauthorized_immigrants_age.csv") %>%
  filter(Data_Year >=2009 & Data_Year <= 2019) 

# 3. Build a Crosswalk for States
fips_map <- c(
  "1"="Alabama", "2"="Alaska", "4"="Arizona", "5"="Arkansas", "6"="California", 
  "8"="Colorado", "9"="Connecticut", "10"="Delaware", "11"="District of Columbia", 
  "12"="Florida", "13"="Georgia", "15"="Hawaii", "16"="Idaho", "17"="Illinois", 
  "18"="Indiana", "19"="Iowa", "20"="Kansas", "21"="Kentucky", "22"="Louisiana", 
  "23"="Maine", "24"="Maryland", "25"="Massachusetts", "26"="Michigan", 
  "27"="Minnesota", "28"="Mississippi", "29"="Missouri", "30"="Montana", 
  "31"="Nebraska", "32"="Nevada", "33"="New Hampshire", "34"="New Jersey", 
  "35"="New Mexico", "36"="New York", "37"="North Carolina", "38"="North Dakota", 
  "39"="Ohio", "40"="Oklahoma", "41"="Oregon", "42"="Pennsylvania", 
  "44"="Rhode Island", "45"="South Carolina", "46"="South Dakota", "47"="Tennessee", 
  "48"="Texas", "49"="Utah", "50"="Vermont", "51"="Virginia", "53"="Washington", 
  "54"="West Virginia", "55"="Wisconsin", "56"="Wyoming"
)
names(totals)[names(totals) == "Data_Year"] <- "year"
names(dhs_states)[names(dhs_states) == "Data_Year"] <- "year"
names(dhs_countries)[names(dhs_countries) == "Data_Year"] <- "year"
names(dhs_states)[names(dhs_states) == "Category_Value"] <- "state_name"
names(dhs_countries)[names(dhs_countries) == "Category_Value"] <- "country_name"
names(dhs_age)[names(dhs_age) == "Category_Value"] <- "age_bracket"


# Translate the ACS state codes into text so it matches the DHS CSV
acs_base <- acs_raw_summary %>%
  mutate(state_name = fips_map[as.character(statefip)])
```

```{r}
# Roll up ACS data to the National level
macro_acs <- acs_base %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    total_foreign_born = sum(total_foreign_born_pop, na.rm = TRUE),
    # Let's use your high probability model for this example
    imputed_count = sum(high_prob_count, na.rm = TRUE) 
  )

# Join and Calculate Rates
macro_comparison <- macro_acs %>%
  # Assuming dhs_totals has columns: year, dhs_unauth_count
  left_join(totals, by = "year") %>%
  mutate(
    imputation_rate = imputed_count / total_foreign_born,
    dhs_rate = Count / total_foreign_born,
    variance = imputation_rate - dhs_rate
  )
```

## Age Composition

To assess whether our machine-learning imputations of undocumented status aligned with the estimates provided by DHS (both derived from the ACS to begin with), we examine the age composition of the undocumented population over the years 2009-2019. We can then compare how closely, or how far, the age composition of our machine-learning undocumented population estimates follow the age composition reported by DHS.

```{r}
#| label: age-bracket-comparison-interactive
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(scales) 
library(plotly) 

# --- 1. CLEAN DHS STRINGS FIRST ---
dhs_age_clean <- dhs_age %>%
  mutate(
    age_bracket = str_replace_all(age_bracket, '=|"', '')
  )

# --- 2. PREP YOUR IMPUTED DATA (All Years) ---
imputed_bracket_dist <- acs_base %>%
  filter(age_bracket != "Under 18") %>% 
  group_by(year, age_bracket) %>%
  summarise(imputed_count = sum(high_recall_count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(year) %>% 
  mutate(
    fraction = imputed_count / sum(imputed_count),
    dataset = "My Imputed ACS" 
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(year, age_bracket, fraction, dataset)

# --- 3. PREP YOUR BENCHMARK DATA (All Years) ---
dhs_age_benchmark <- dhs_age_clean %>%
   filter(age_bracket != "Under 18") %>%
   group_by(Data_Year, age_bracket) %>%
   summarise(bench_count = sum(Count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
   group_by(Data_Year) %>% 
   mutate(
     fraction = bench_count / sum(bench_count),
     dataset = "DHS Official"
   ) %>%
   ungroup() %>%
   rename(year = Data_Year) %>% 
   select(year, age_bracket, fraction, dataset)

# Combine both datasets for plotting 
plot_data <- bind_rows(imputed_bracket_dist, dhs_age_benchmark) %>%
  mutate(
    age_bracket = factor(age_bracket, levels = c("18-24", "25-34", "35-44", "45-54", "55 And Over"))
  )

# --- 4. BUILD THE ANIMATED PLOT ---
p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = age_bracket, y = fraction, fill = dataset, frame = year)) +
  # Removed explicit width formatting here; we will let Plotly handle it
  geom_col(position = "dodge", color = "black", alpha = 0.85) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("DHS Official" = "#4472C4", "My Imputed ACS" = "#ED7D31")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  
  labs(
    x = "Age Bracket",
    y = "Percentage of Adult Population", 
    fill = "" 
  ) +
  
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray80", size = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", size = 1, fill = NA)
    # Removed legend.position = "bottom" from ggplot; Plotly will handle it
  )

# Convert to interactive chart and force Plotly to fix the formatting
ggplotly(p, tooltip = c("fill", "y", "frame")) %>%
  layout(
    # FIX 1: Force Plotly to group the bars and set specific, thick widths
    barmode = "group",
    bargap = 0.2,       # The space between age brackets
    bargroupgap = 0.05, # The tiny space between the DHS and ACS bars
    
    # FIX 2: Safely position the legend below the x-axis title
    legend = list(
      orientation = "h",
      x = 0.5,
      xanchor = "center",
      y = -0.15 
    ),
    
    # Add bottom margin to make room for everything
    margin = list(b = 100) 
  ) %>%
  animation_opts(frame = 1000, transition = 500, redraw = TRUE) %>%
  animation_slider(
    currentvalue = list(prefix = "Year: ", font = list(color = "black")),
    # Push the slider down slightly so it doesn't overlap the new legend
    pad = list(t = 40)
  )
```
